title: bassia laniflora
intro: Bassia laniflora, also known as Alangium or Yangmugua, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Bassia laniflora is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's extracts and compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-viral, and anti-cancer properties, making it a potential natural remedy for improving immune function and preventing chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Bassia laniflora often include its roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, or be processed into capsules and extracts for oral consumption.
